heading: Carbon capture technology program
label: 16292 Carbon capture technology program
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 16292, , , , related to coal and related technologies program, prior to repeal by , , .Pub. L. 109–58, title IX, § 962119 Stat. 890Pub. L. 116–260, div. Z, title IV, § 4002(a)134 Stat. 2528Aug. 8, 2005Dec. 27, 2020

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–58, div. D, title III, § 40301135 Stat. 986Nov. 15, 2021“Congress finds that—“(1)the industrial sector is integral to the economy of the United States—“(A)providing millions of jobs and essential products; and“(B)demonstrating global leadership in manufacturing and innovation;“(2)carbon capture and storage technologies are necessary for reducing hard-to-abate emissions from the industrial sector, which emits nearly 25 percent of carbon dioxide emissions in the United States;“(3)carbon removal and storage technologies, including direct air capture, must be deployed at large-scale in the coming decades to remove carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere;“(4)large-scale deployment of carbon capture, removal, utilization, transport, and storage—“(A)is critical for achieving mid-century climate goals; and“(B)will drive regional economic development, technological innovation, and high-wage employment;“(5)carbon capture, removal, and utilization technologies require a backbone system of shared carbon dioxide transport and storage infrastructure to enable large-scale deployment, realize economies of scale, and create an interconnected carbon management market;“(6)carbon dioxide transport infrastructure and permanent geological storage are proven and safe technologies with existing Federal and State regulatory frameworks;“(7)carbon dioxide transport and storage infrastructure share similar barriers to deployment previously faced by other types of critical national infrastructure, such as high capital costs and chicken-and-egg challenges, that require Federal and State support, in combination with private investment, to be overcome; and“(8)each State should take into consideration, with respect to new carbon dioxide transportation infrastructure—“(A)qualifying the infrastructure as pollution control devices under applicable laws (including regulations) of the State; and“(B)establishing a waiver of ad valorem and property taxes for the infrastructure for a period of not less than 10 years.”

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–58, div. D, title III, § 40301135 Stat. 986Nov. 15, 2021“Congress finds that—“(1)the industrial sector is integral to the economy of the United States—“(A)providing millions of jobs and essential products; and“(B)demonstrating global leadership in manufacturing and innovation;“(2)carbon capture and storage technologies are necessary for reducing hard-to-abate emissions from the industrial sector, which emits nearly 25 percent of carbon dioxide emissions in the United States;“(3)carbon removal and storage technologies, including direct air capture, must be deployed at large-scale in the coming decades to remove carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere;“(4)large-scale deployment of carbon capture, removal, utilization, transport, and storage—“(A)is critical for achieving mid-century climate goals; and“(B)will drive regional economic development, technological innovation, and high-wage employment;“(5)carbon capture, removal, and utilization technologies require a backbone system of shared carbon dioxide transport and storage infrastructure to enable large-scale deployment, realize economies of scale, and create an interconnected carbon management market;“(6)carbon dioxide transport infrastructure and permanent geological storage are proven and safe technologies with existing Federal and State regulatory frameworks;“(7)carbon dioxide transport and storage infrastructure share similar barriers to deployment previously faced by other types of critical national infrastructure, such as high capital costs and chicken-and-egg challenges, that require Federal and State support, in combination with private investment, to be overcome; and“(8)each State should take into consideration, with respect to new carbon dioxide transportation infrastructure—“(A)qualifying the infrastructure as pollution control devices under applicable laws (including regulations) of the State; and“(B)establishing a waiver of ad valorem and property taxes for the infrastructure for a period of not less than 10 years.”

children.label: A
children.text: represents the scale of technology development beyond laboratory development and bench scale testing, but not yet advanced to the point of being tested under real operational conditions at commercial scale;

children.label: B
children.text: represents the scale of technology necessary to gain the operational data needed to understand the technical and performance risks of the technology before the application of that technology at commercial scale or in commercial-scale demonstration; and

children.label: i
children.text: to validate scaling factors; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to demonstrate the interaction between major components so that control philosophies for a new process can be developed and enable the technology to advance from large-scale pilot project application to commercial-scale demonstration or application.

children.label: A
children.text: natural gas;

children.label: B
children.text: liquid petroleum gas;

children.label: C
children.text: synthetic gas derived from petroleum or natural gas liquids;

children.label: D
children.text: any mixture of natural gas and synthetic gas; or

children.label: E
children.text: biomethane.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “natural gas electric generation facility” means a facility that generates electric energy using natural gas as the fuel.

children.label: i
children.text: simple cycle plant;

children.label: ii
children.text: combined cycle plant;

children.label: iii
children.text: combined heat and power plant; or

children.label: iv
children.text: steam methane reformer that produces hydrogen from natural gas for use in the production of electric energy.

children.heading: Program
children.label: 4 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the program established under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “transformational technology” means a technology that represents a significant change in the methods used to convert energy that will enable a step change in performance, efficiency, cost of electricity, and reduction of emissions as compared to the technology in existence on .December 27, 2020

children.label: I
children.text: advanced combustion systems, including oxygen combustion systems and chemical looping; and

children.label: II
children.text: the replacement of steam cycles with supercritical carbon dioxide cycles;

children.label: ii
children.text: improvements in steam or carbon dioxide turbine technology;

children.label: iii
children.text: improvements in carbon capture, utilization, and storage systems technology;

children.label: iv
children.text: improvements in small-scale and modular coal-fired technologies with reduced carbon output or carbon capture that can support incremental power generation capacity additions;

children.label: v
children.text: fuel cell technologies for low-cost, high-efficiency modular power systems;

children.label: vi
children.text: advanced gasification systems;

children.label: vii
children.text: thermal cycling technologies; and

children.label: viii
children.text: any other technology the Secretary recognizes as transformational technology.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a carbon capture technology program for the development of transformational technologies that will significantly improve the efficiency, effectiveness, costs, emissions reductions, and environmental performance of coal and natural gas use, including in manufacturing and industrial facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: a research and development program;

children.label: B
children.text: large-scale pilot projects;

children.label: C
children.text: demonstration projects, in accordance with paragraph (4);

children.label: D
children.text: a front-end engineering and design program for carbon capture technologies; and

children.label: E
children.text: a front-end engineering and design program for carbon dioxide transport infrastructure necessary to enable deployment of carbon capture, utilization, and storage technologies.

children.label: i
children.text: ensuring reliable, low-cost power from new and existing coal electric generation facilities and natural gas electric generation facilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: achieving high conversion efficiencies;

children.label: iii
children.text: addressing emissions of carbon dioxide and other air pollutants;

children.label: iv
children.text: developing small-scale and modular technologies to support incremental capacity additions and load following generation, in addition to large-scale generation technologies;

children.label: v
children.text: supporting dispatchable operations for new and existing applications of coal and natural gas generation; and

children.label: vi
children.text: accelerating the development of technologies that have transformational energy conversion characteristics.

children.label: i
children.text: accelerating the development, deployment, and commercialization of technologies to capture and sequester carbon dioxide emissions from new and existing coal electric generation facilities and natural gas electric generation facilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: supporting sites for safe geological storage of large volumes of anthropogenic sources of carbon dioxide and the development of the infrastructure needed to support a carbon dioxide utilization and storage industry;

children.label: iii
children.text: improving the conversion, utilization, and storage of carbon dioxide produced from fossil fuels and other anthropogenic sources of carbon dioxide;

children.label: iv
children.text: lowering greenhouse gas emissions for all fossil fuel production, generation, delivery, and use, to the maximum extent practicable;

children.label: v
children.text: developing carbon utilization technologies, products, and methods, including carbon use and reuse for commercial application;

children.label: vi
children.text: developing net-negative carbon dioxide emissions technologies; and

children.label: vii
children.text: developing technologies for the capture of carbon dioxide produced during the production of hydrogen from natural gas.

children.label: i
children.text: further reducing non-carbon dioxide air emissions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: reducing the use, and managing the discharge, of water in power plant operations.

children.label: i
children.text: nontraditional fuel manufacturing facilities, including ethanol or other biofuel production plants or hydrogen production plants; and

children.label: ii
children.text: energy-intensive manufacturing facilities that produce carbon dioxide as a byproduct of operations.

children.label: E
children.text: Entering into cooperative agreements to carry out and expedite demonstration projects (including pilot projects) to demonstrate the technical and commercial viability of technologies to reduce carbon dioxide emissions released from coal electric generation facilities and natural gas electric generation facilities for commercial deployment.

children.label: F
children.text: Identifying any barriers to the commercial deployment of any technologies under development for the capture of carbon dioxide produced by coal electric generation facilities and natural gas electric generation facilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out the program, the Secretary shall establish a demonstration program under which the Secretary, through a competitive, merit-reviewed process, shall enter into cooperative agreements by not later than , for demonstration projects to demonstrate the construction and operation of 6 facilities to capture carbon dioxide from coal electric generation facilities, natural gas electric generation facilities, and industrial facilities.September 30, 2025

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: B Technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary, to the maximum extent practicable, shall provide technical assistance to any eligible entity seeking to enter into a cooperative agreement described in subparagraph (A) for the purpose of obtaining any necessary permits and licenses to demonstrate qualifying technologies.

children.heading: Eligible entities
children.label: C Eligible entities
children.text: The Secretary may enter into cooperative agreements under subparagraph (A) with industry stakeholders, including any industry stakeholder operating in partnership with the National Laboratories, institutions of higher education, multiinstitutional collaborations, and other appropriate entities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In carrying out the program, the Secretary shall establish a carbon capture technology commercialization program to demonstrate substantial improvements in the efficiency, effectiveness, cost, and environmental performance of carbon capture technologies for power, industrial, and other commercial applications.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: ii Requirement
children.text: The program established under clause (i) shall include funding for commercial-scale carbon capture technology demonstrations of projects supported by the Department, including projects in addition to the projects described in subparagraph (A), including funding for not more than 2 projects to demonstrate substantial improvements in a particular technology type beyond the first of a kind demonstration and to account for considerations described in subparagraph (G).

children.label: i
children.text: 2 shall be designed to capture carbon dioxide from a natural gas electric generation facility;

children.label: ii
children.text: 2 shall be designed to capture carbon dioxide from a coal electric generation facility; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 2 shall be designed to capture carbon dioxide from an industrial facility not purposed for electric generation.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be designed to further the development, deployment, and commercialization of technologies to capture and sequester carbon dioxide emissions from new and existing coal electric generation facilities, natural gas electric generation facilities, and industrial facilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: shall be financed in part by the private sector; and

children.label: iii
children.text: if necessary, shall secure agreements for the offtake of carbon dioxide emissions captured by qualifying technologies during the project.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: To be eligible to enter into an agreement with the Secretary for a demonstration project under subparagraphs (A) and (D), an entity shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: I
children.text: ensure a broad geographic distribution of project sites;

children.label: II
children.text: ensure that a broad selection of electric generation facilities are represented;

children.label: III
children.text: ensure that a broad selection of technologies are represented; and

children.label: IV
children.text: leverage existing public-private partnerships and Federal resources.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct, and submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives a report on the results of, a study of the successes, failures, practices, and improvements of the Department in carrying out demonstration projects under this paragraph.December 27, 2020

children.label: aa
children.text: applicant and contractor qualifications;

children.label: bb
children.text: project management practices at the Department;

children.label: cc
children.text: economic or market changes and other factors impacting project viability;

children.label: dd
children.text: completion of third-party agreements, including power purchase agreements and carbon dioxide offtake agreements;

children.label: ee
children.text: regulatory challenges; and

children.label: ff
children.text: construction challenges.

children.label: I
children.text: consider any relevant recommendations, as determined by the Secretary, provided in the report required under clause (i)(I); and

children.label: II
children.text: adopt such recommendations as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: a list of any activities carried out by the Secretary to solicit or evaluate the applications; and

children.label: II
children.text: a process for ensuring that any projects carried out under a cooperative agreement entered into under subparagraph (A) are designed to result in the development or demonstration of qualifying technologies.

children.label: I
children.text: in the first report required under clause (i), a detailed list of technical milestones for the development and demonstration of each qualifying technology pursued under the demonstration program established under subparagraph (A);

children.label: II
children.text: in each subsequent report required under clause (i), a description of the progress made towards achieving the technical milestones described in subclause (I) during the applicable period covered by the report; and

children.label: aa
children.text: an estimate of the cost of licensing, permitting, constructing, and operating each carbon capture facility expected to be constructed under the demonstration program established under subparagraph (A);

children.label: bb
children.text: a schedule for the planned construction and operation of each demonstration or pilot project under the demonstration program; and

children.label: cc
children.text: an estimate of any financial assistance, compensation, or incentives proposed to be paid by the host State, Indian Tribe, or local government with respect to each facility described in item (aa).

children.heading: Intraagency coordination for carbon capture, utilization, and sequestration activities
children.label: 5 Intraagency coordination for carbon capture, utilization, and sequestration activities
children.text: The carbon capture, utilization, and sequestration activities described in paragraph (3)(B) shall be carried out by the Assistant Secretary for Fossil Energy, in coordination with the heads of other relevant offices of the Department and the National Laboratories.

children.label: A
children.text: undertake international collaborations, taking into consideration the recommendations of the National Coal Council and the National Petroleum Council;

children.label: B
children.text: use existing authorities to encourage international cooperation; and

children.label: i
children.text: coal and natural gas producers;

children.label: ii
children.text: industries that use coal and natural gas;

children.label: iii
children.text: organizations that promote coal, advanced coal, and natural gas technologies;

children.label: iv
children.text: environmental organizations;

children.label: v
children.text: organizations representing workers; and

children.label: vi
children.text: organizations representing consumers.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report describing the program goals and objectives adopted under subsection (b)(3).December 27, 2020

children.heading: Update
children.label: 2 Update
children.text: Not less frequently than once every 2 years after the initial report is submitted under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report describing the progress made towards achieving the program goals and objectives adopted under subsection (b)(3).

children.label: i
children.text: $230,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 and 2022; and

children.label: ii
children.text: $150,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2025;

children.label: i
children.text: $225,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 and 2022;

children.label: ii
children.text: $200,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 and 2024; and

children.label: iii
children.text: $150,000,000 for fiscal year 2025;

children.label: i
children.text: $500,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 though 2024; and

children.label: ii
children.text: $600,000,000 for fiscal year 2025;

children.label: D
children.text: for activities under the front-end engineering and design program described in subsection (b)(2)(D), $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2024; and

children.label: E
children.text: for activities under the front-end engineering and design program described in subsection (b)(2)(E), $100,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.

children.heading: Cost sharing for large-scale pilot projects
children.label: 2 Cost sharing for large-scale pilot projects
children.text: Activities under subsection (b)(2)(B) shall be subject to the cost-sharing requirements of .section 16352(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall award grants to 1 or more entities for the operation of 1 or more test centers (referred to in this subsection as a “Center”) to provide distinct testing capabilities for innovative carbon capture technologies.December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: advance research, development, demonstration, and commercial application of carbon capture technologies;

children.label: B
children.text: support large-scale pilot projects and demonstration projects and test carbon capture technologies; and

children.label: C
children.text: develop front-end engineering design and economic analysis.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall select entities to receive grants under this subsection according to such criteria as the Secretary may develop.

children.heading: Competitive basis
children.label: B Competitive basis
children.text: The Secretary shall select entities to receive grants under this subsection on a competitive basis.

children.label: i
children.text: have access to existing or planned research facilities for carbon capture technologies;

children.label: ii
children.text: are institutions of higher education with established expertise in engineering for carbon capture technologies, or partnerships with such institutions of higher education; or

children.label: iii
children.text: have access to existing research and test facilities for bulk materials design and testing, component design and testing, or professional engineering design.

children.heading: Existing centers
children.label: D Existing centers
children.text: In selecting entities to receive grants under this subsection, the Secretary shall prioritize carbon capture test centers in existence on .December 27, 2020

children.heading: Formula for awarding grants
children.label: 4 Formula for awarding grants
children.text: The Secretary may develop a formula for awarding grants under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each grant awarded under this subsection shall be for a term of not more than 5 years, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: B Renewal
children.text: The Secretary may renew a grant for 1 or more additional 5-year terms, subject to a competitive merit review and the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 6 Termination
children.text: To the extent otherwise authorized by law, the Secretary may eliminate, and terminate grant funding under this subsection for, a Center during any 5-year term described in paragraph (5) if the Secretary determines that the Center is underperforming.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 7 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.
